lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: What am I to do?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's wrong?

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Oh dear, oh dear, oh dear, I'm in a terrible terrible mess! It's the Duke's birthday today, and I should be making him a lovely big birthday cake.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: I've forgotten to buy the ingredients. I'll never get them in time now. He'll sack me! What will I do? I have four children and a goat to look after. Would you help me? Please?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I'll help you.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Oh thank you, thank you. I need milk, an egg and flour. I'd be very grateful if you can get them for me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have all of those ingredients on me already!

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: That's an odd coincidence... Were you planning on making a cake too?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not exactly. I just had an odd feeling you might be needing these ingredients. If I see a cook, I presume there's food of some kind! Lucky guess I suppose.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Well thank you! Hand them over, please.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: You've brought me everything I need! I am saved! Thank you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So do I get to go to the Duke's Party?

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: I'm afraid not, only the big cheeses get to dine with the Duke.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, maybe one day I'll be important enough to sit on the Duke's table.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Maybe, but I won't be holding my breath.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So where do I find these ingredients then?

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: There is a Mill fairly close, go North and then West. Mill Lane Mill is just off the road to Draynor. I usually get my flour from there.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Talk to Millie, she'll help, she's a lovely girl and a fine Miller. Make sure you take a pot with you for the flour though, you've got one on you already.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Talk to Millie, she'll help, she's a lovely girl and a fine Miller. Make sure you take a pot with you for the flour though, there should be one on the table in here.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: There is a cattle field on the other side of the river, just across the road from the Groats' Farm.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Talk to Gillie Groats, she looks after the Dairy cows - she'll tell you everything you need to know about milking cows!

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: You'll need an empty bucket for the milk itself. I do see you've got a bucket with you already luckily!

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: You'll need an empty bucket for the milk itself. The general store just north of the castle will sell you one for a couple of coins.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: I normally get my eggs from the Groats' farm, on the other side of the river.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: But any chicken should lay eggs.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've got all the information I need. Thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I know where to find this stuff.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I don't feel like it. Maybe later.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Fine. I always knew you Adventurer types were callous beasts. Go on your merry way!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're a cook, why don't you bake me a cake?

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: *sniff* Don't talk to me about cakes...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's wrong?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You don't look very happy.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: No, I'm not. The world is caving in around me - I am overcome by dark feelings of impending doom.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd take the rest of the day off if I were you.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: No, that's the worst thing I could do. I'd get in terrible trouble.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well maybe you need to take a holiday...

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: That would be nice, but the Duke doesn't allow holidays for core staff.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm, why not run away to the sea and start a new life as a Pirate?

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: My wife gets sea sick, and I have an irrational fear of eyepatches. I don't see it working myself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid I've run out of ideas.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: I know I'm doomed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's wrong?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nice hat!

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Err thank you. It's a pretty ordinary cooks hat really.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Still, suits you. The trousers are pretty special too.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Its all standard cook's issue uniform...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The whole hat, apron, stripey trousers ensemble - it works. It make you looks like a real cook.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: I am a real cook! I haven't got time to be chatting about Culinary Fashion. I am in desperate need of help!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's wrong?

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: How are you getting on with finding the ingredients?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I haven't got any of them yet, I'm still looking.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Please get the ingredients quickly - I'm running out of time! The Duke will throw me into the streets!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So where do I find these ingredients then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here's a bucket of milk.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here's a pot of flour.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here's a fresh egg.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Thanks for the ingredients you have got so far. Please get the rest quickly - I'm running out of time! The Duke will throw me into the streets!

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: You've brought me everything I need! I am saved! Thank you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So do I get to go to the Duke's Party?

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: I'm afraid not, only the big cheeses get to dine with the Duke.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, maybe one day I'll be important enough to sit on the Duke's table.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Maybe, but I won't be holding my breath.
